name: Meltano
what_it_does: Open-source Singer-based ELT CLI that orchestrates taps (extractors), targets (loaders), and mappers using a declarative meltano.yml config with built-in state management for incremental loads.
use_cases: Extract data from SaaS APIs (Stripe, Salesforce, GitHub) using Singer taps and load into a warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, Postgres) with a single `meltano run tap-X target-Y` command
Manage incremental extraction state automatically — Meltano persists Singer bookmarks between runs so subsequent extractions only fetch new or updated records
Integrate dbt transformations into the same pipeline: run `meltano run tap-stripe target-postgres dbt-postgres:run` to extract, load, and transform in one orchestrated sequence
Manage plugin versions and configs as code in meltano.yml, enabling GitOps workflows where pipeline definitions are reviewed, versioned, and deployed like application code
Test and validate new Singer connectors locally before deploying to production by running `meltano run --dry-run` to verify configuration without touching the destination
not_for: Real-time or streaming ingestion — Meltano is batch-oriented; Singer taps poll APIs on a schedule, not event-driven
Complex in-flight transformations during extraction — the EL step is intentionally dumb; transformation belongs in the T layer (dbt) after loading
Teams that need a managed SaaS solution with a UI — Meltano is CLI-first and self-hosted; use Airbyte Cloud or Fivetran if you need a managed connector platform with web UI
best_when: You want code-first, GitOps-managed ELT pipelines with Singer's broad connector ecosystem, incremental state management, and the flexibility to extend with custom taps or dbt transformations.
avoid_when: Your team lacks Python/CLI comfort, you need a managed SaaS platform, or your sources require streaming semantics — Meltano's batch model and self-hosted ops overhead are significant.

auth.notes: No Meltano-level auth. Individual tap/target plugins handle their own auth (API keys, OAuth tokens, service account credentials) configured in meltano.yml or via environment variables.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully open source and self-hosted with no restrictions
pricing.paid_tiers: Meltano Cloud (managed) offered commercial tiers; check meltano.com for current availability
pricing.notes: Core Meltano is MIT-licensed. Connector plugins (Singer taps/targets) are separately licensed, most are open source. Hosting and infrastructure costs are borne by the operator.

performance.rate_limits: None (CLI tool); rate limits are imposed by upstream tap source APIs

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Meltano surfaces plugin errors through Singer protocol stderr — error messages come from the tap/target plugin itself and vary widely in quality depending on the connector author.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: EL runs are idempotent for full syncs if the target supports upserts. Incremental runs with state bookmarks are not safely replayable without resetting state — agents must explicitly reset state for re-extraction.
known_agent_gotchas: Plugin state is stored per environment and job name — agents running the same pipeline in different environments (dev/prod) have separate state, so a failed prod run cannot be resumed from dev state

meltano.yml is the source of truth for all plugin configs; environment variables override settings at runtime but are not persisted — agents must write config changes to meltano.yml for them to survive restarts

Singer catalog schema is negotiated between tap and target at runtime; schema mismatches (tap emits a field the target doesn't accept) cause silent data drops or hard failures depending on the target's error handling

The EL distinction is strict — mappers must be explicitly added to the pipeline if in-flight transformations are needed; placing transformation logic in a tap or target is an antipattern that breaks reusability

Plugin installation via `meltano add` resolves versions from the Meltano Hub at install time; pinning plugin versions in meltano.yml is critical for reproducible pipelines, as unpinned plugins can receive breaking updates silently

security_score_components.security_notes: Meltano itself has no network-facing surface. Secrets should be passed via environment variables rather than hardcoded in meltano.yml. Plugin supply chain risk exists as taps/targets are third-party packages — dependency auditing is recommended.
